troubleshooting.title: Debug a rule that never passes on a valid document
troubleshooting.shortdesc: A rule that always fails even on correctly formatted documents usually has a mismatch between the query result and the expected value. Use the REPL to inspect the exact values the rule is working with.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Debug a markdown-validator rule that fails on every document even when the document satisfies the rule's intent. Common causes: whitespace, wrong operator, multi-element XPath, wrong flag.

condition.title: Situation
condition.p: You have a rule that you believe should pass on a correctly formatted document, but it always reports FAIL regardless of the document content.

cause.title: Cause: Whitespace in the query result
cause.p: The metadata value or XPath text result has leading or trailing whitespace that prevents an equality match.

step.cmd: Start the REPL, load the document, and inspect the exact value.

info.codeblock: md-validate repl > load docs/article.md > get ms.topic

step.stepresult: Compare the output to what your rule's value field expects. Look for extra spaces or quotes.

step.cmd: Update the rule's value field to match the exact string, or clean up the front matter.

cause.title: Cause: Multi-element XPath result with equality operator
cause.p: An XPath expression returns multiple elements, and the == operator requires every element to match the expected value. If any element differs, the rule fails. This is almost never the intended behavior.

step.cmd: In the REPL, run the query with the text flag to see all results.

info.codeblock: > query //h2 text Result: ['Prerequisites', 'Deploy the app', 'Clean up resources']

step.cmd: Narrow the XPath to target the specific element you want by position.

info.codeblock: # Instead of: //h2[last()]/preceding-sibling::h2 # Use: > query //h2[last()-1] text Result: ['Deploy the app']

cause.title: Cause: Wrong flag for the check type
cause.p: Using text when you need count, or vice versa, causes the comparison to fail because the result is not in the expected form.

step.cmd: Review the flags reference to confirm you are using the correct flag for your check.

info.p: The flags reference lists all flags with descriptions and examples.

step.cmd: In the REPL, compare the output of different flags on the same XPath to see which form matches your expected value.

info.codeblock:
> query /html/body/h1 count Result: 1
> query /html/body/h1 text Result: ['How to deploy the app']
info.p: Use count when comparing to a number, and text when comparing to a string.

cause.title: Cause: Inverted workflow branch logic
cause.p: A workflow intended to express absence uses a branch pattern that is inverted — it passes when the content is wrong and fails when the content is correct.

step.cmd: Run validation with --verbose to trace the workflow step-by-step.

info.p: The verbose output shows which branch (True or False) the decision node follows for each workflow step.

step.cmd: Read the decision point output carefully and verify your branch routing.

info.p: The True branch runs when the precondition rule passes. If your workflow uses F-sentinel for the False branch, confirm the sentinel rule passes on valid documents by testing it in the REPL with eval.
Rule design patterns covers the absence-check pattern and the known fragility of workflow inversion.
